title: The cleaner of a small temple
summary: Describe an old man who quietly cleans a small temple every morning.
topic: The cleaner of a small temple
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about a person who cleans a small temple, church, or mosque. Say what time he works, what he does, and why his work matters. Use present simple.
hintStarters: Near my flat in Bangkok, there is a small temple and…
Every morning at five, an old man…
He sweeps the steps slowly because…
sampleText: Near my flat in Bangkok, there is a small wooden temple between two tall buildings. Every morning at five, an old man comes to clean it. I see him from my window when I cannot sleep. He carries a long bamboo broom and a small plastic bucket. He sweeps the steps slowly. He moves every leaf to the side of the path. He does not hurry. He wipes the wooden door with a wet cloth. He cleans around the old candles. He picks up the ends of yesterday's incense. He puts them in the bucket. He never throws anything on the street. He does not talk much. Sometimes he says one short word to a cat that sits on the wall. The first people come to pray at six. By that time, the temple is clean and ready. The old man stands at the side. He bows his head a little. Then he walks home with his broom. He does this every day, even when it rains. I do not know his name. But I think his quiet work is a kind of love.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tàn nhang của ngày hôm trước

sampleAnnotations.phrase: The first people come to pray
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: những người đầu tiên đến cầu nguyện

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for daily work
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi mô tả công việc hàng ngày của ai đó, dùng hiện tại đơn ngôi thứ ba. Nhớ thêm -s hoặc -es: 'comes', 'carries', 'sweeps', 'wipes', 'cleans', 'puts', 'walks'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: For someone's daily work, use third-person present simple. Remember to add -s or -es: 'comes', 'carries', 'sweeps', 'wipes', 'cleans', 'puts', 'walks'.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Prepositions of place: 'near', 'between', 'on', 'at'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'near my flat' (gần), 'between two buildings' (giữa hai), 'on the wall' (trên), 'at five' (lúc). Mỗi giới từ có nghĩa riêng — đừng dịch trực tiếp từ tiếng Việt.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'near my flat' (close to), 'between two buildings' (in the middle of two), 'on the wall' (on a surface), 'at five' (a clock time). Each preposition has its own meaning — do not translate word by word.

sampleTranslationVi: Gần căn hộ của tôi ở Bangkok, có một ngôi đền gỗ nhỏ giữa hai tòa nhà cao. Mỗi sáng lúc năm giờ, một ông cụ đến dọn dẹp. Tôi thấy ông từ cửa sổ khi không ngủ được. Ông mang một cây chổi tre dài và một xô nhựa nhỏ. Ông quét bậc thang chậm rãi. Ông gạt từng chiếc lá sang bên đường. Ông không vội vàng. Ông lau cửa gỗ bằng khăn ướt. Ông dọn quanh những cây nến cũ. Ông nhặt tàn nhang của ngày hôm trước. Ông bỏ vào xô. Ông không bao giờ vứt thứ gì ra đường. Ông không nói nhiều. Đôi khi ông nói một từ ngắn với con mèo ngồi trên tường. Những người đầu tiên đến cầu nguyện lúc sáu giờ. Đến lúc đó, đền đã sạch và sẵn sàng. Ông cụ đứng sang một bên. Ông cúi đầu nhẹ. Rồi ông đi về với cây chổi. Ông làm việc này mỗi ngày, kể cả khi trời mưa. Tôi không biết tên ông. Nhưng tôi nghĩ công việc thầm lặng của ông là một dạng tình yêu.

options: Near the writer's flat, between two tall buildings
On a hill far from the city
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'there is a small wooden temple between two tall buildings'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What time does the old man arrive?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'Every morning at five, an old man comes to clean it'.
The text says 'He carries a long bamboo broom and a small plastic bucket'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does he do with the old incense?
options: He throws it on the street.
He picks it up and puts it in the bucket.
He gives it to the cat.
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'He picks up the ends of yesterday's incense. He puts them in the bucket'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Who does he sometimes talk to?
options: A cat that sits on the wall
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'sometimes he says one short word to a cat that sits on the wall'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does the writer think of the cleaner's work?
options: It is a kind of love.
It is a waste of time.
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I think his quiet work is a kind of love'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which third-person present form is correct?
Choose the correct preposition: 'There is a temple ___ two tall buildings.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'between' is used for two things on each side.
mcQuestions.hint: Two buildings on each side of the temple.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'at' for a clock time?
Which sentence is in the correct present simple negative?
mcQuestions.explanation: Third person negative uses 'does not + base verb': 'does not talk'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do we often pay more attention to people who pray than to people who clean the place of prayer?
